ClinicalDocument.title: Mental Health and Substance Misuse Specialist Services Care Programme Approach Review Summary

section2.text: Tony often uses a nickname 'Maca'.
section2.title: Aliases or Other Names

section2.text: Tony needs his hearing aid to hear clearly - a visit from an Audiologist has provided Tony with a new rechargeable hearing aid - although he does still need support to ensure he keeps it recharged and cleaned. Native Language Irish.
section2.title: Communication and Comprehension

section2.text: Formally Separated.
section2.title: Marital or Partnership Status

section2.text: Squatting. Living in a Communal Setting: This is a squat that Tony shares with 5 other people. The Squat has recently been served with an eviction order and Tony will not be able to return to this address
section2.title: Housing

section3.text: The Squat is not accessible and its residents use barricades to prevent anyone getting in to evict them.
section3.title: Property Access Information

section2.text:
Tony has expressed a wish to attend the Church of the Virgin Mary, Uptown some Sunday mornings. Ethnicity: White Irish.
Religion Philosophy: Roman Catholic Church.
section2.title: Ethnicity, Religion, Philosophy & Culture

section2.text: Tony has recently been attending a local IT beginner’s course having expressed an interest in learning about computers.
section2.title: Education, Training, Employment and Financial Status

section3.text: Before admission to Hospital Tony was in receipt of Jobseekers Allowance, he has since been in receipt of Incapacity Benefit 02/04/2004 – ongoing.
section3.title: Financial Status

COCD_TP146061UK01.Section1.title: Referral, First Contact, Triage and Entry

section2.text: Referral Date: 01/01/2004
section2.title: Referral / First Contact

section3.text: Tony was expressing some grandiose ideas about who he is that lead staff at the hostel to become concerned for him. He can be aggressive and personally invasive, however he had not been observed being physically violent.
section3.title: Reason for Referral / First Contact

section2.text: Date Recorded: 01/01/2004. URGENT. The referral indicates that it is important that Tony is seen urgently.
section2.title: Triage Decision

section2.text: Date of Entry: 01/01/2004
section2.title: Specialist Service Assessment

section3.text: Tony was expressing grandiose ideas and was aggressive and invasive.
section3.title: Presenting Problems

section2.text: Entry Date: 01/01/2004
section2.title: Entry to MH&SM Specialist Services

section3.text: The decision was taken to admit Tony for assessment and treatment
section3.title: Specialist Services Entry Decision Summary

section2.text: This is Tony's new address for the period of his detention.
section2.title: Further Details or Concerns Relating to Current Contact Status

COCD_TP146061UK01.Section1.title: Care Teams or Organisations Involved in Care Delivery

section2.text: Date when the first entry for this team was recorded = 04/05/2004.
section2.title: Main Care Team

section3.title: Involvement in Patient Care or Support

section2.text: Date when the first entry for this team was recorded = 03/03/2004
section2.title: Additional Care Team

section3.text: Uptown Mental Health Trust Occupational Therapy Team - assisting Tony in some of his domestic and socialising activities.
section3.title: Involvement in Patient Care or Support

tr.td:
Tony's Mother - Margaret undertook the role of Nearest Relative when he was sectioned.
Carer ID: 98765432

MHA Nearest Relative - Tony's Mother.
Last assessment conducted on 09/02/2004.

Andrew is Tony's Older Brother and provides all the support for his financial and legal affairs and will advocate on his behalf for all activities where advocacy may be required.
Carer ID: 23456789
Person Holding a Power of Attornay.

Shelter Hostel
Jones Street
Leeds
LS1 1AA
Andrew Smith is the Hostel Manager for Shelter. He has a good relationship with Tony and is able to get through to him sometimes when he is otherwise difficult to engage with.
Care Coordinator/Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker at Uptown Social Services

COCD_TP146061UK01.Section1.title: Persons with a Connection to the Care of Patient

section3.text: All the participants agreed to the conclusions of the review and its outcomes
section3.title: Agreement of HSCPs / Carers to Review and Conclusions

section3.text:
The following aspects of care are detailed in the care components section of this review:
Medication management
Accommodation difficulties
Lack of structure to day / meaningful occupation
Financial difficulties
Treatment and monitoring of bipolar disorder
Management of drug and alcohol misuse
section3.title: Summary of Review Outcomes
Patient / Service User Involvement in Review

section4.text: Tony declined to attend the review, however I was able to see him the same day in the evening.
section4.title: Patients Involvement In The Review

section4.text: Tony accepted the new care plan and said he was particularly looking forward to the social worker helping him to find somewhere better to live for when he is discharged.
section4.title: Patient / Service User Agreement to Review

section4.text: Ward Office in patient paper record.
section4.title: Location of Signed Paper Copy of Agreement
Means used to provide a Summary Copy of the review for the Patient / Service User

section2.text: Tony has a hearing problem for which he has seen an Audiologist and has been fitted with appropriate new hearing aids.
section2.title: Registered Disability / Other Condition(s)

section3.text: Suggest Tony attend a hearing clinic to determine the exact cause of his hearing problem as well as further care required.
section3.title: Care Treatment Support Required

section2.text:
Primary Substance: Alcohol.
Age of first use = 12.
Taken Orally
section2.title: Problem Substances Information

tr.td: MHA Department HEBBURN MENTAL HEALTH TRUST CAMPBELL PARK ROAD TYNE & WEAR NE31 2SP on 01/01/2004 at 11:30.
End of the period where papers authorised for enacting a section may be served:

section3.text: Following some bizarre comments and expressions by Tony the drop-in centre manager called the local Social Services Department who instigated this MHA Assessment.
section3.title: Reason for MHA Assessment

section3.text: It was agreed that Tony requires further assessment and so an application for a Section was made.
section3.title: Outcome of Assessment

section2.text: Summary: Detained in hospital under Section 3 of the Mental Health Act 1983 (England and Wales) as of 01/01/2004 : 10:30.
section2.title: Legal Status and MHA Details

section3.text: Tony is to be detained at Uptown Inpatient Mental Health Unit.
section3.title: MHA Section Conditions

list.item: Lack of structure to day / meaningful occupation
Treatment and monitoring of bipolar disorder
Management of drug and alcohol misuse

section2.text:
Tony needs to take medication regularly to treat his mental illness.
Aims and Objectives:
Tony to take medication regularly and safely.
Tony to be prescribed appropriate amounts of medication that successfully treat his illness and keep side effects to a minimum.
Interventions & Actions:
Education regarding need to take medication regularly. /Information about medication used and how to take and store it safely.
Regular monitoring of efficacy and side effects.
Regular blood tests for Clozapine prescriptions - blood results sent to Clozapine monitoring service.
Blood tests for lithium levels.
Prescriptions for clozapine to be issued by CMHT medical staff following clearance from clozapine monitoring service.
All other prescriptions to be issued by GP.
Liaison with community pharmacist to ensure their understanding of clozapine prescription process and regular supply of medication.
Regular monitoring of weight, blood pressure and blood sugar levels.
Education regarding need to maintain a healthy lifestyle, including exercise regime. GP to be advised of responsibility.
Frequency of Contact:
Weekly visits from CMHN.
6 weekly outpatient appointments with Consultant Psychiatrist.
Attendance at Clozapine clinic for blood tests - variable frequency depending on results.
Fortnightly attendances at GP surgery to see practice nurse.
Weekly prescriptions from GP to be collected by Tony from local pharmacy.
Care Plan component Start Date: 03/03/2004
Date for this Care Plan component Review:
01/09/2004
Care Plan Component Review Status: Review Not Reached
GP will have responsibility for this component.
section2.title: Care Plan Component: Medication Management

section2.text:
Tony was living in a Squat before he was admitted; there is a need to support him to find more appropriate accommodation.
Aims and Objectives:
To find suitable accommodation - possibly with one of the local housing associations.
Interventions & Actions:
Ask Social Worker - Joan Harper to support Tony in this.
Care Plan component Start Date: 03/03/2004
Date for this Care Plan component Review:
01/09/2004
Care Plan Component Review Status: Review Not Reached
section2.title: Care Plan Component: Accommodation

tr.td: Tony stays the day with Andrew his older brother

section2.text: If Tony deteriorates the Uptown CMHT should be contacted to see if further care is needed. This will have to be treated as a new care spell and an MHA Assessment will need doing - if not done already.
section2.title: Contingency Plan

section2.text: Increased use of Alcohol. Hypomania symptoms. Irritability.
section2.title: Relapse Indicators

section2.text: In the event that Tony absconds, contact the Police.
section2.title: Crisis Plan

tr.td: Tony has not shown any indications that he is suicidal or that his mood is severely depressed.
He has not shown any indications of intentional self-harm.
Tony can be clumsy and his ability to move quickly has been affected by the consumption of alcohol over time.
During particularly heavy drinking bouts, Tony may go for days without attention to personal hygiene, appearance or proper dietary intake.
Tony is at his most vulnerable when under the influence of alcohol, however his aggressive behaviour does tend to ward-off anyone attempting to approach him.
Tony becomes verbally abusive when he doesn't get his way. Although he has not actually been physically violent he can be extremely intimidating.
When under the influence of alcohol, Tony can be extremely aggressive - this aggression has caused him to be arrested several times for breaches of the peace and using threatening behaviour.
The level of abuse is stricly limited to the effects of alcohol upon him - he does seem to manipulate or hurt others intentionally.
There are no persons dependent upon Tony at this time, however it would be unlikely that he could take on any such responsibilities.

section3.title: Risk Assessment Categories and Scale Scores

section4.text:
Category:
H1 Previous Violence
Scale: 1
Indications have recently come to our attention that in his younger life Tony was convicted of Breach of the Peace connected to criminal activities - however there has not been any recent evidence of violent behaviours.
section4.title: HCR20 Outcome Score

tr.td: When he has had a considerable amount of alcohol he can be verbally aggressive.

section2.text:
Date: 01/05/2002
Category: Aggression to Adults
Level: High
Tony has a long history of aggressive behaviour which whilst it has not seemed to extend into physical violence. He was arrested for a breach of the peace in May 2002 and was bound over to keep the peace at that time.
section2.title: Risk Activities History

tr.td: HoNOS item 10 - activities of daily living

COCD_TP146061UK01.Section1.text: No formal mental health history previously This care episode commenced on 01/01/04, with Tony admitted with indications of Hypomania.
COCD_TP146061UK01.Section1.title: Additional Information

section2.title: Aspect of Care that a Preference relates to.

section3.text: Tony does not want his younger brother Frederick to know anything about his condition.
section3.title: Stated Preference Information
